evidence: Docstring states that an infinite set with distinct consecutive-term sums 'has lower density 0', formalized via Set.lowerDensity.
Docstring conjectures that such a set 'has density 0', formalized via Set.HasDensity.
Docstring: 'When A corresponds to the set of primes, it is conjectured that the limsup of the number of representations ... is infinite', using Nat.nth Nat.Prime.
Docstring: 'When A corresponds to the set of primes ... the set of numbers n that have representations ... has positive upper density'.
File header cites 'Wikipedia: Minimum overlap problem' and the decl states the central question, the limit of the minimum-max-overlap quotient M(N)/N.
Docstring asks for the largest set A in {1,...,cn} 'such that n is not a sum of a subset of A', i.e. avoiding a subset-sum representation of n.
Docstring asks whether only finitely many disjoint interval collections have product of all elements 'a square', formalized with IsSquare.
Docstring: 'There is no consecutive triple of powerful numbers', stated via Nat.Powerful.
Docstring: Erdos conjectured for the k-th powerful number n_k that n_{k+2} - n_k > n_k^c, formalized with Nat.nth Powerful.
Docstring asks 'Are there any 2-full n such that n+1 is 3-full?'; 2-full number is an exact alias of powerful number and k-full numbers are its standard generalization.
Docstring asks for infinitely many n with largest prime factor of n below n^{1/2} and of n+1 below (n+1)^{1/2}, i.e. consecutive sqrt-smooth integers.
